records.ety: [Aphetic form of OE. aventure. See Adventure.]

defs: An undertaking of chance or danger; the risking of something upon an event which can not be foreseen with certainty; a hazard; a risk; a speculation.
quotes: I, in this venture, double gains pursue.

defs: An event that is not, or can not be, foreseen; an accident; chance; hap; contingency; luck.
The thing put to hazard; a stake; a risk; especially, something sent to sea in trade.
quotes: My ventures are not in one bottom trusted.
A certain man drew a bow at a venture.
A bargain at a venture made.
notes: ☞ The phrase at a venture was originally at aventure, that is, at adventure.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Ventured (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Venturing.]

defs: To hazard one's self; to have the courage or presumption to do, undertake, or say something; to dare.
To make a venture; to run a hazard or risk; to take the chances.
quotes: Who freights a ship to venture on the seas.

defs: To expose to hazard; to risk; to hazard; as, to venture one's person in a balloon.
quotes: I am afraid; and yet I'll venture it.

defs: To put or send on a venture or chance; as, to venture a horse to the West Indies.
To confide in; to rely on; to trust.
quotes: A man would be well enough pleased to buy silks of one whom he would not venture to feel his pulse.
